chief_complaints: Patient presents with a {duration}-day history of {symptom}.
{age}-year-old {sex} with chief complaint of {symptom} for the past {duration} days.
Reports new-onset {symptom} beginning approximately {duration} days ago.
Follow-up visit for ongoing management of {chronic_condition}.
hpi_phrases: Pain is described as {quality}, rated {severity}/10, and is {pattern}.
Symptoms are exacerbated by {trigger} and relieved by {reliever}.
Patient reports compliance with current medication regimen.
No prior episodes of similar symptoms.
Symptoms have progressively worsened despite home management.
ros_systems: Constitutional: Denies fever, chills, or weight changes.
HEENT: No vision changes, sore throat, or congestion.
Cardiovascular: Denies chest pain, palpitations, or edema.
Respiratory: Denies cough, wheezing, or shortness of breath at rest.
Gastrointestinal: Denies nausea, vomiting, diarrhea, or constipation.
Genitourinary: Denies dysuria, frequency, or hematuria.
Musculoskeletal: Reports occasional joint stiffness; denies acute injury.
Neurological: Denies headache, dizziness, or focal weakness.
Psychiatric: Denies depression, anxiety, or suicidal ideation.
Endocrine: Denies polyuria, polydipsia, or heat/cold intolerance.
Hematologic/Lymphatic: Denies easy bruising or lymphadenopathy.
physical_exam: General: Alert, oriented x3, in no acute distress.
Vitals: BP {bp_sys}/{bp_dia}, HR {hr}, RR {rr}, Temp {temp} F, SpO2 {spo2}%.
HEENT: Normocephalic, atraumatic. PERRL. Oropharynx clear.
Neck: Supple, no JVD, no thyromegaly, no lymphadenopathy.
Respiratory: Clear to auscultation bilaterally, no wheezes or rales.
Abdomen: Soft, non-tender, non-distended, normoactive bowel sounds.
Extremities: No clubbing, cyanosis, or edema. Pulses 2+ throughout.
Skin: Warm, dry, intact. No rashes or lesions.
Neurological: CN II-XII grossly intact, strength 5/5 throughout, sensation intact.
assessment_phrases: Symptoms suggestive of {condition}; further workup indicated.
Continue current management; reassess in {followup_weeks} weeks.
Patient counseled on lifestyle modifications including diet and exercise.
medications: Albuterol inhaler 90 mcg 2 puffs Q4H PRN
